questions.question: The Panchayat system is based on the principles of which of the following Acts?
options: The Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996
The West Bengal Land Reforms Act, 1967
questions.explanation: The Panchayat system is based on the principles of the West Bengal Panchayat Act, 1973.

questions.question: Article 355 of the Indian Constitution deals with the responsibility of the
options: President of India towards the State Governments
State Governments towards the Local Bodies
questions.explanation: Article 355 of the Indian Constitution deals with the responsibility of the Centre towards the State Governments.

questions.question: The first State to implement the Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution was
questions.explanation: The first State to implement the Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution was Tripura.

questions.question: The concept of Fundamental Duties of citizens is incorporated in the
questions.explanation: The concept of Fundamental Duties of citizens is incorporated in the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976.

questions.question: The West Bengal Panchayat Elections (Amendment) Act, 2021 provides for the reservation of 50% seats in the Panchayats for women
options: in all three tiers of Panchayats
in two lower tiers of Panchayats
only in the district level Panchayats
only in the gram panchayat level
questions.explanation: The West Bengal Panchayat Elections (Amendment) Act, 2021 provides for the reservation of 50% seats in the Panchayats for women in all three tiers of Panchayats.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers forms the border between the Indian states of West Bengal and Bihar?
questions.explanation: The Ganga River forms the border.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest island in the Sundarbans delta in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Sagardwip Island is the largest island.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges is located in the northern part of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas are located in the northern part.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest lake in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Kalash River Lake is the largest lake.

questions.question: Which of the following districts in West Bengal is known as the 'Land of Mangrove Forest'?
questions.explanation: South 24 Parganas is known as the 'Land of Mangrove Forest'.

questions.question: The Mamata Banerjee government in West Bengal launched a new scheme to provide financial assistance to women entrepreneurs in the state. Which scheme was launched?
questions.explanation: The Udayan Scheme was launched to provide financial assistance to women entrepreneurs.

questions.question: Which river in West Bengal has been affected by the recent floods in the state?
questions.explanation: The Ganges river has been affected by the recent floods in West Bengal.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has announced a new policy to promote renewable energy in the state. What is the name of this policy?
questions.explanation: The West Bengal Renewable Energy Policy was announced to promote renewable energy in the state.

questions.question: Which of the following festivals was celebrated with great enthusiasm in West Bengal in April 2026?
questions.explanation: Saraswati Puja was celebrated with great enthusiasm in West Bengal in April 2026.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has launched a new initiative to promote digital literacy in the state. What is the name of this initiative?
questions.explanation: The Digital Bengal Initiative was launched to promote digital literacy in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes of the West Bengal government aims to provide financial assistance to women who have been victims of acid attacks?
questions.explanation: It provides financial assistance to women who have been victims of acid attacks.

questions.question: What is the name of the West Bengal government's scheme for providing health insurance to the people of the state?
questions.explanation: It provides health insurance to the people of the state.

questions.question: The West Bengal government's 'Sabooj Sangha' scheme aims to provide assistance to which of the following groups?
questions.explanation: It aims to provide assistance to minority communities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key component of the West Bengal government's 'Palli Sakshya Abhijan' scheme?
questions.explanation: It focuses on literacy programs for children.

questions.question: The West Bengal government's 'Pathar Ghora' scheme aims to provide assistance to which of the following groups?
questions.explanation: It aims to provide assistance to women and children.

questions.question: A fruit vendor in West Bengal sells apples at a profit of 10%. If the selling price of an apple is Rs. 18, what was the cost price of the apple?
questions.explanation: Cost price is the original price of an item before the profit is added.

questions.question: A shopkeeper in Kolkata buys a TV at a discount of 12% from the market price of Rs. 25000. What amount did the shopkeeper pay for the TV?
questions.explanation: Buying price is the price after the discount has been subtracted from the original price.

questions.question: A company in West Bengal sells 1000 units of a product at a profit of 20%. If the total revenue generated is Rs. 80000, what is the cost price of each unit?
questions.explanation: Cost price is the original price of an item before the profit is added.

questions.question: A person in West Bengal invests Rs. 8000 in a business. If the annual profit is 15%, what is the total amount of profit earned in a year?
questions.explanation: Profit is the return on an investment.

questions.question: A local vendor in West Bengal sells a product at a loss of 5%. If the selling price is Rs. 105, what is the cost price of the product?
questions.explanation: Cost price is the original price of an item before the loss is subtracted.

questions.question: A person deposits ₹ 5000 in a bank account, which earns compound interest at the rate of 8% per annum compounded annually. What will be the amount at the end of 2 years?
questions.explanation: Amount can be calculated using the compound interest formula A = P(1 + r)^n.

questions.question: A bank offers a simple interest of 4% per annum on a deposit. What is the total interest earned by a deposit of ₹ 6000 for 3 years?
questions.explanation: Interest can be calculated using the simple interest formula I = (P * r * t) / 100.

questions.question: If the compound interest for 2 years on a certain sum of money at 10% per annum is ₹ 550, what will be the simple interest earned on the same sum at 5% per annum for 5 years?
questions.explanation: Simple interest can be calculated using the simple interest formula I = (P * r * t) / 100.

questions.question: What is the difference in the compound interest and simple interest earned on a deposit of ₹ 8000 at 6% per annum for 2 years?
questions.explanation: Compound interest is calculated using the compound interest formula A = P(1 + r)^n.

questions.question: A sum of money at compound interest at the rate of 12% per annum for 3 years amounts to ₹ 12000. What is the sum?
questions.explanation: Amount can be calculated using the compound interest formula A = P(1 + r)^n.

questions.question: A bus travelling from Kolkata to Durgapur covers a distance of 180 km in 6 hours. What is the speed of the bus?
questions.explanation: Speed is distance divided by time.

questions.question: A train from Sealdah to Howrah travels at an average speed of 40 kmph. How much time will it take to cover a distance of 120 km?
questions.explanation: Time is distance divided by speed.

questions.question: A car travels from Esplanade to Gariahat at an average speed of 25 kmph. If it covers a certain distance in 4 hours, how much distance will it cover in 8 hours?
questions.explanation: Distance is speed multiplied by time.

questions.question: A bus from Howrah to Budge Budge takes 3 hours to cover a certain distance. If the average speed of the bus is 30 kmph, how much distance does it cover?
questions.explanation: Distance is speed multiplied by time.

questions.question: A train travels from Park Circus to Tollygunge at an average speed of 50 kmph. If it takes 2 hours to cover a certain distance, how much distance will it cover?
questions.explanation: Distance is speed multiplied by time.

questions.question: A certain code is given: B × F = C, C + D = E, E - B = A. If B = 3, find the value of A.
questions.explanation: The code is based on simple arithmetic operations.

questions.question: In a certain code, F is the code for 'Friend', H is the code for 'Hello', and the code for 'Hello Friend' is H F. What is the code for 'Friend Hello'?
questions.explanation: The code is based on the order of words.

questions.question: In a certain code, 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 0 represents the numbers 0 to 9. If the code for 'twenty five' is 2 5 8 1, what is the code for 'twenty one'?
questions.explanation: The code is based on the numerical values of the digits.

questions.question: If in a certain code, 1 = @, 2 = #, 3 = $, 4 = %, 5 = ^, then what is the code for 'house' if the code for 'use' is u#e and 'house' is related to 'use'?
questions.explanation: The code is based on analogy.

questions.question: In a certain code, if F = 1, E = 2, D = 3, C = 4, B = 5, A = 6, then what will be the code for 'seven'?
questions.explanation: The code is based on alphabetical order.

questions.question: If A and B are siblings, and C is the mother of A and B, then what is the relationship of C to B?
questions.explanation: C is the mother of A and B.

questions.question: A says that B is the son of her mother's husband. How is B related to A?
questions.explanation: B is the husband of A's mother.

questions.question: In a family, A is the father of B, and B is the father of C. What is the relationship of A to C?
questions.explanation: A is the grandfather of C.

questions.question: X says that Y is the daughter of her father's brother. What is the relationship of Y to X?
questions.explanation: Y is the sister of X's mother.

questions.question: A says that B is the daughter of her father's wife's father. How is B related to A?
questions.explanation: B is the grandmother of A.

questions.question: A man standing at the bank of Hooghly River sees a boat moving towards him. If the speed of the boat in still water is 5 km/hr and the river is flowing at 2 km/hr, what direction will the man see the boat after 20 minutes?
questions.explanation: The boat will be moving in the direction of the river's flow.

questions.question: If a person is moving towards the north and takes a left turn, which direction will he be facing?
questions.explanation: He will be facing the east direction.

questions.question: A person starts walking from Sealdah Railway Station and takes 8 turns to the left. Which direction will he be facing now?
questions.explanation: He will be facing the south direction.

questions.question: If a person is standing at the Howrah Bridge and sees a train coming from the south, which direction will the train be moving?
questions.explanation: The train will be moving towards the west.

questions.question: A person walks 20 meters towards the east and then turns right. He walks 15 meters. Now, if he turns left and walks 30 meters, which direction will he be facing?
questions.explanation: He will be facing the north direction.

questions.question: If the pattern continues, the next number in the series 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, ? is:
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is obtained by adding 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and so on, to the previous number.

questions.question: What is the next letter in the series G, H, J, K, M ?
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is obtained by adding 1, 2, 2, 1, to the corresponding alphabets.

questions.question: If the pattern continues, the next number in the series 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ? is:
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is obtained by adding 4, 6, 8, 10 to the previous number.

questions.question: What is the next number in the series 14, 24, 34, 44, 54, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is obtained by adding 10 to each number.

questions.question: What is the next letter in the series A, D, G, J, M ?
questions.explanation: The pattern in the series is obtained by adding 3, 3, 3, 3 to the corresponding alphabets.
